design_philosophy.core_loop: Select stage, navigate platforming sections with precise jumping, defeat boss enemy using collected special weapons (gas types), repeat across 6 stages then final fortress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity of special ammo combined with pattern-heavy boss encounters; player must decide when to use limited special gas projectiles vs. standard gas gun
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Darkwing Duck using gadget-based problem solving (gas gun variants) to defeat rogues gallery of themed enemies; cape deflection mechanic reinforces defensive counter-play fantasy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict Mega Man formula adaptation with Disney IP: stage-select freedom combined with required weapon variety management. Single special gas equipped at a time creates meaningful tactical choice. Cape deflection is unique traversal/defensive tool. No lives system permissiveness—designed as deliberate mechanical constraint

difficulty.lives_system: Health bar (heart reserve shown in HUD); appears to be single-life with damage accumulation, following NES platformer conventions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through weapon choice: player can switch between standard gas and three special gas types (Thunder, Heavy, Arrow) freely via Select button. This creates self-imposed difficulty through resource management decisions

progression.upgrade_system: Three special gas weapon types (Thunder, Heavy, Arrow) found throughout stages; each has different ammunition cost and function. Player carries one special gas type at a time; switching replaces previous weapon
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage select with branching: three stages available initially, second set of three unlocked after first completion. Final F.O.W.L. Floating Fortress stage unlocked after all six stages cleared
replayability_hooks: Low% (No sub-weapons) speedrun category indicates viable pacifist/minimalist playstyle
Multiple special gas types encourage different tactical approaches per stage
Stage select order creates variable difficulty routing
Cape deflection mechanic allows advanced players to avoid certain enemy projectiles entirely

world_building_techniques: Rogues gallery structure: six themed villain stages (Liquidator, Megavolt, Bushroot, Quackerjack, Professor Moliarty, Wolfduck) tied to specific areas of St. Canard
Climactic fortress confrontation with Steelbeak following stage progression
License adherence: original character Wolfduck created specifically for game to expand Darkwing's rogues gallery

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-heavy encounters mimicking Mega Man boss structure; six distinct villain bosses each thematically tied to their stage (e.g., Liquidator in liquid-themed level). Final Steelbeak confrontation as series climax

faction_indicators: Six hired criminals working under F.O.W.L. umbrella suggest organizational hierarchy
danger_signals: Crime wave across St. Canard establishes stakes
Steelbeak as valued F.O.W.L. operative indicates threat level escalation
environmental_storytelling: Stage themes tied to villain identities (Liquidator controls water areas, Megavolt controls electrical zones, etc.)
Progression from criminal hideouts to F.O.W.L. Floating Fortress shows enemy organization spatial hierarchy
Multiple themed zones throughout St. Canard create sense of city under siege

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor (development notes on Mega Man 5 basis, unused content)
Wikipedia (gameplay mechanics, boss roster, narrative plot summary, release dates)
